description: Worklist for the threat-actor-updater task. Other tasks queue items here when they encounter threat actors that need new pages or linkage updates. The updater processes this queue on Tue/Thu.

queue.context: Salt Typhoon breached FBI DCSNet surveillance system (DCS-3000/Red Hook) via ISP vendor supply chain compromise. Classified as FISMA major incident April 2026.

FulcrumSec exploited React2Shell vulnerability to breach LexisNexis AWS infrastructure, exfiltrating 2.04GB of data including government user profiles. Known for targeting cloud infrastructure and public shaming/data leaks.
Mr. Raccoon breached Adobe through an Indian BPO contractor using RAT deployment and spear-phishing, exfiltrating 13M support tickets, 15K employee records, and HackerOne bug bounty submissions.

ShinyHunters (UNC5537) compromised Hims & Hers Zendesk via Okta SSO and maintains active data leak site. Known for vishing, SaaS platform targeting, and credential theft operations against Snowflake, Salesforce customers.
Russian-speaking ransomware group that compromised Die Linke political party, claiming 1.5TB of data. Financially and politically motivated. Party described attack as potential hybrid warfare.

Iran-linked hacktivist group (Void Manticore / MOIS) that wiped 200,000+ Stryker devices across 79 countries via compromised Microsoft Intune. Motivated by retaliation for Feb 28 missile strike on Iranian school. Shifting from espionage to destructive operations.

Also tracked as UNC6780, DeadCatx3, PCPcat, ShellForce. Executed cascading supply chain campaign against Trivy, KICS, LiteLLM, Telnyx. CanisterWorm spread across 47+ npm packages. Partnership with CipherForce and Vect ransomware groups. Estimated 300GB credentials and 500K machine compromises.

UNC4736 subgroup attributed to $285M Drift Protocol DeFi exploit. 6-month social engineering campaign using VSCode/Cursor vulnerability and malicious TestFlight app. Durable nonce attack mechanism bypassed multisig governance. Also linked to $50M Radiant Capital hack.

Medusa ransomware group attacked Passaic County NJ, demanding $800K ransom. Known for double extortion and rapid exploitation — Microsoft reports they can move from initial access to deployment within 24 hours, including zero-day exploitation.

EvilTokens is a Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform launched mid-February 2026 via NOIRLEGACY GROUP Telegram channel. Powers OAuth device code phishing campaign targeting 340+ M365 organizations across nine countries. Templates impersonate Microsoft, Adobe, DocuSign, SharePoint. Used by Storm-2372, APT29, UTA0304, UTA0307.
Storm-2372 identified as one of the threat groups using EvilTokens PhaaS for M365 OAuth device code phishing campaign targeting 340+ organizations.
RansomHouse (Jolly Scorpius) is a data extortion / RaaS group active since Dec 2021 with 187+ victims. Uses MarioLocker and MrAgent. Gap-fill report created.

actions.create: Threat actor not found in threat-actor-index.json — needs a new entry in threat-actors.html with full research (aliases, affiliation, motivation, tools, campaigns, description)
actions.update-links: Threat actor exists but a new incident report references them — update their campaigns/description and ensure bidirectional linking
actions.enrich: Threat actor exists but has thin data — research and expand their entry

priority.critical: Actor tied to active/ongoing campaign affecting critical infrastructure
priority.high: Actor tied to multiple recent incidents in the manifest
priority.low: Historical actor with no recent activity, fill when capacity allows

queueRules.dedup: Before adding to queue, check if an item with the same actorName and action already exists. If so, merge referencedFrom arrays instead of adding a duplicate.
